metadata.preview:
package action

// Type defines a type for the Actions of Resources
// actions are also stored as a lookup db table named iam_action
type Type int

// not using iota intentionally, since the values are stored in the db as well.
const (
	Unknown          Type = 0
	List             Type = 1
	Create           Type = 2
	Update           Type = 3
	Read             Type = 4
	Delete           Type = 5
	Authenticate     Type = 6
	All              Type = 7
	Authorize        Type = 8
	AddGrants        Type = 9
	RemoveGrants     Type = 10
	SetGrants        Type = 11

9: func TestAction(t *testing.T) {
10: 	tests := []struct {
11: 		action Type

15: 			action: Unknown,
16: 			want:   "unknown",
17: 		},
18: 		{
19: 			action: List,
20: 			want:   "list",
21: 		},
22: 		{
23: 			action: Create,
24: 			want:   "create",
25: 		},

28: 			want:   "update",
29: 		},
30: 		{

32: 			want:   "read",
33: 		},
34: 		{

36: 			want:   "delete",

40: 			want:   "authenticate",
41: 		},
42: 		{
43: 			action: All,
44: 			want:   "*",
45: 		},
46: 		{
47: 			action: Authorize,
48: 			want:   "authorize",
49: 		},
50: 		{
51: 			action: AddGrants,
52: 			want:   "add-grants",
53: 		},
54: 		{
55: 			action: RemoveGrants,
56: 			want:   "remove-grants",
57: 		},
58: 		{
59: 			action: SetGrants,
60: 			want:   "set-grants",
61: 		},
62: 		{

64: 			want:   "add-principals",
65: 		},

74: 		{

79: 	for _, tt := range tests {
80: 		t.Run(tt.want, func(t *testing.T) {

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package perms
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"strings"
5: 	"testing"
6: 
7: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/types/action"
8: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/types/resource"
9: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/types/scope"
10: 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
11: 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
12: )
13: 
14: func Test_ActionParsingValidation(t *testing.T) {
15: 	t.Parallel()
16: 
17: 	type input struct {
18: 		name      string
19: 		input     Grant
20: 		errResult string
21: 		result    Grant
22: 	}
23: 
24: 	tests := []input{
25: 		{
26: 			name:      "no actions",
27: 			errResult: "no actions specified",
28: 		},
29: 		{
30: 			name: "empty action",
31: 			input: Grant{
32: 				actionsBeingParsed: []string{"create", "", "read"},
33: 			},
34: 			errResult: "empty action found",
35: 		},
36: 		{
37: 			name: "unknown action",
38: 			input: Grant{
39: 				actionsBeingParsed: []string{"create", "foobar", "read"},
40: 			},
41: 			errResult: `unknown action "foobar"`,
42: 		},
43: 		{
44: 			name: "all",
45: 			input: Grant{
46: 				actionsBeingParsed: []string{"*"},
47: 			},
48: 			result: Grant{
49: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
50: 					action.All: true,
51: 				},
52: 			},
53: 		},
54: 		{
55: 			name: "all valid plus all",
56: 			input: Grant{
57: 				actionsBeingParsed: []string{"list", "create", "update", "*", "read", "delete", "authenticate", "authorize"},
58: 			},
59: 			errResult: `"*" cannot be specified with other actions`,
60: 		},
61: 		{
62: 			name: "all valid",
63: 			input: Grant{
64: 				actionsBeingParsed: []string{"list", "create", "update", "read", "delete", "authenticate", "authorize"},
65: 			},
66: 			result: Grant{
67: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
68: 					action.List:         true,
69: 					action.Create:       true,
70: 					action.Update:       true,
71: 					action.Read:         true,
72: 					action.Delete:       true,
73: 					action.Authenticate: true,
74: 					action.Authorize:    true,
75: 				},
76: 			},
77: 		},
78: 	}
79: 
80: 	for _, test := range tests {
81: 		t.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {
82: 			err := test.input.parseAndValidateActions()
83: 			if test.errResult == "" {
84: 				require.NoError(t, err)
85: 				assert.Equal(t, test.result, test.input)
86: 			} else {
87: 				require.Error(t, err)
88: 				assert.Equal(t, test.errResult, err.Error())
89: 			}
90: 		})
91: 	}
92: }
93: 
94: func Test_ValidateType(t *testing.T) {
95: 	t.Parallel()
96: 
97: 	type input struct {
98: 		name      string
99: 		input     Grant
100: 		errResult string
101: 	}
102: 
103: 	tests := []input{
104: 		{
105: 			name: "no specifier",
106: 		},
107: 		{
108: 			name: "valid specifier",
109: 			input: Grant{
110: 				typ: resource.HostCatalog,
111: 			},
112: 		},
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	for _, test := range tests {
116: 		t.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {
117: 			err := test.input.validateType()
118: 			if test.errResult == "" {
119: 				require.NoError(t, err)
120: 			} else {
121: 				require.Error(t, err)
122: 				assert.Equal(t, test.errResult, err.Error())
123: 			}
124: 		})
125: 	}
126: }
127: 
128: func Test_MarshalingAndCloning(t *testing.T) {
129: 	t.Parallel()
130: 
131: 	type input struct {
132: 		name            string
133: 		input           Grant
134: 		jsonOutput      string
135: 		canonicalString string
136: 	}
137: 
138: 	tests := []input{
139: 		{
140: 			name: "empty",
141: 			input: Grant{
142: 				scope: Scope{
143: 					Type: scope.Org,
144: 				},
145: 			},
146: 			jsonOutput:      `{}`,
147: 			canonicalString: ``,
148: 		},
149: 		{
150: 			name: "type and id",
151: 			input: Grant{
152: 				id: "baz",
153: 				scope: Scope{
154: 					Type: scope.Project,
155: 				},
156: 				typ: resource.Group,
157: 			},
158: 			jsonOutput:      `{"id":"baz","type":"group"}`,
159: 			canonicalString: `id=baz;type=group`,
160: 		},
161: 		{
162: 			name: "everything",
163: 			input: Grant{
164: 				id: "baz",
165: 				scope: Scope{
166: 					Type: scope.Project,
167: 				},
168: 				typ: resource.Group,
169: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
170: 					action.Create: true,
171: 					action.Read:   true,
172: 				},
173: 				actionsBeingParsed: []string{"create", "read"},
174: 			},
175: 			jsonOutput:      `{"actions":["create","read"],"id":"baz","type":"group"}`,
176: 			canonicalString: `id=baz;type=group;actions=create,read`,
177: 		},
178: 	}
179: 
180: 	for _, test := range tests {
181: 		t.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {
182: 			output, err := test.input.MarshalJSON()
183: 			require.NoError(t, err)
184: 			assert.Equal(t, test.jsonOutput, string(output))
185: 			assert.Equal(t, test.canonicalString, test.input.CanonicalString())
186: 			assert.Equal(t, &test.input, test.input.clone())
187: 		})
188: 	}
189: }
190: 
191: func Test_Unmarshaling(t *testing.T) {
192: 	t.Parallel()
193: 
194: 	type input struct {
195: 		name      string
196: 		jsonInput string
197: 		textInput string
198: 		jsonErr   string
199: 		textErr   string
200: 		expected  Grant
201: 	}
202: 
203: 	tests := []input{
204: 		{
205: 			name:      "empty",
206: 			expected:  Grant{},
207: 			jsonInput: `{}`,
208: 			textInput: ``,
209: 			textErr:   `segment "" not formatted correctly, wrong number of equal signs`,
210: 		},
211: 		{
212: 			name:      "bad json",
213: 			jsonInput: `w329uf`,
214: 			jsonErr:   "invalid character 'w' looking for beginning of value",
215: 		},
216: 		{
217: 			name:      "bad segment",
218: 			jsonInput: `{"id":true}`,
219: 			jsonErr:   `unable to interpret "id" as string`,
220: 			textInput: `id=`,
221: 			textErr:   `segment "id=" not formatted correctly, missing value`,
222: 		},
223: 		{
224: 			name: "good id",
225: 			expected: Grant{
226: 				id: "foobar",
227: 			},
228: 			jsonInput: `{"id":"foobar"}`,
229: 			textInput: `id=foobar`,
230: 		},
231: 		{
232: 			name:      "bad id",
233: 			jsonInput: `{"id":true}`,
234: 			jsonErr:   `unable to interpret "id" as string`,
235: 			textInput: `=id`,
236: 			textErr:   `segment "=id" not formatted correctly, missing key`,
237: 		},
238: 		{
239: 			name: "good type",
240: 			expected: Grant{
241: 				typ: resource.HostCatalog,
242: 			},
243: 			jsonInput: `{"type":"host-catalog"}`,
244: 			textInput: `type=host-catalog`,
245: 		},
246: 		{
247: 			name:      "bad type",
248: 			jsonInput: `{"type":true}`,
249: 			jsonErr:   `unable to interpret "type" as string`,
250: 			textInput: `type=host-catalog=id`,
251: 			textErr:   `segment "type=host-catalog=id" not formatted correctly, wrong number of equal signs`,
252: 		},
253: 		{
254: 			name: "good actions",
255: 			expected: Grant{
256: 				actionsBeingParsed: []string{"create", "read"},
257: 			},
258: 			jsonInput: `{"actions":["create","read"]}`,
259: 			textInput: `actions=create,read`,
260: 		},
261: 		{
262: 			name:      "bad actions",
263: 			jsonInput: `{"actions":true}`,
264: 			jsonErr:   `unable to interpret "actions" as array`,
265: 			textInput: `type=host-catalog=id`,
266: 			textErr:   `segment "type=host-catalog=id" not formatted correctly, wrong number of equal signs`,
267: 		},
268: 		{
269: 			name:      "empty actions",
270: 			jsonInput: `{"actions":[""]}`,
271: 			jsonErr:   `empty action found`,
272: 			textInput: `actions=,`,
273: 			textErr:   `empty action found`,
274: 		},
275: 		{
276: 			name:      "bad json action",
277: 			jsonInput: `{"actions":[1, true]}`,
278: 			jsonErr:   `unable to interpret 1 in actions array as string`,
279: 		},
280: 	}
281: 
282: 	for _, test := range tests {
283: 		t.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {
284: 			assert := assert.New(t)
285: 			require := require.New(t)
286: 			var g Grant
287: 			if test.jsonInput != "" {
288: 				err := g.unmarshalJSON([]byte(test.jsonInput))
289: 				if test.jsonErr != "" {
290: 					require.Error(err)
291: 					assert.Equal(test.jsonErr, err.Error())
292: 				} else {
293: 					require.NoError(err)
294: 					assert.Equal(test.expected, g)
295: 				}
296: 			}
297: 			g = Grant{}
298: 			if test.textInput != "" {
299: 				err := g.unmarshalText(test.textInput)
300: 				if test.textErr != "" {
301: 					require.Error(err)
302: 					assert.Equal(test.textErr, err.Error())
303: 				} else {
304: 					require.NoError(err)
305: 					assert.Equal(test.expected, g)
306: 				}
307: 			}
308: 		})
309: 	}
310: }
311: 
312: func Test_Parse(t *testing.T) {
313: 	t.Parallel()
314: 
315: 	type input struct {
316: 		name          string
317: 		input         string
318: 		userId        string
319: 		accountId     string
320: 		err           string
321: 		scopeOverride string
322: 		expected      Grant
323: 	}
324: 
325: 	tests := []input{
326: 		{
327: 			name: "empty",
328: 			err:  `grant string is empty`,
329: 		},
330: 		{
331: 			name:  "bad json",
332: 			input: "{2:193}",
333: 			err:   `unable to parse JSON grant string:`,
334: 		},
335: 		{
336: 			name:  "bad text",
337: 			input: "id=foo=bar",
338: 			err:   `unable to parse grant string:`,
339: 		},
340: 		{
341: 			name:  "bad type",
342: 			input: "id=foobar;type=barfoo;actions=create,read",
343: 			err:   `unknown type specifier "barfoo"`,
344: 		},
345: 		{
346: 			name:  "bad actions",
347: 			input: "id=foobar;type=host-catalog;actions=createread",
348: 			err:   `unknown action "createread"`,
349: 		},
350: 		{
351: 			name:  "empty id and type",
352: 			input: "actions=create",
353: 			err:   `parsed grant string would not result in any action being authorized`,
354: 		},
355: 		{
356: 			name:  "good json type",
357: 			input: `{"type":"host-catalog","actions":["create"]}`,
358: 			expected: Grant{
359: 				scope: Scope{
360: 					Id:   "o_scope",
361: 					Type: scope.Org,
362: 				},
363: 				typ: resource.HostCatalog,
364: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
365: 					action.Create: true,
366: 				},
367: 			},
368: 		},
369: 		{
370: 			name:  "good json id",
371: 			input: `{"id":"foobar","actions":["read"]}`,
372: 			expected: Grant{
373: 				scope: Scope{
374: 					Id:   "o_scope",
375: 					Type: scope.Org,
376: 				},
377: 				id:  "foobar",
378: 				typ: resource.Unknown,
379: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
380: 					action.Read: true,
381: 				},
382: 			},
383: 		},
384: 		{
385: 			name:  "good text type",
386: 			input: `type=host-catalog;actions=create`,
387: 			expected: Grant{
388: 				scope: Scope{
389: 					Id:   "o_scope",
390: 					Type: scope.Org,
391: 				},
392: 				typ: resource.HostCatalog,
393: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
394: 					action.Create: true,
395: 				},
396: 			},
397: 		},
398: 		{
399: 			name:  "good text id",
400: 			input: `id=foobar;actions=read`,
401: 			expected: Grant{
402: 				scope: Scope{
403: 					Id:   "o_scope",
404: 					Type: scope.Org,
405: 				},
406: 				id:  "foobar",
407: 				typ: resource.Unknown,
408: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
409: 					action.Read: true,
410: 				},
411: 			},
412: 		},
413: 		{
414: 			name:          "default project scope",
415: 			input:         `id=foobar;actions=read`,
416: 			scopeOverride: "p_1234",
417: 			expected: Grant{
418: 				scope: Scope{
419: 					Id:   "p_1234",
420: 					Type: scope.Project,
421: 				},
422: 				id:  "foobar",
423: 				typ: resource.Unknown,
424: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
425: 					action.Read: true,
426: 				},
427: 			},
428: 		},
429: 		{
430: 			name:          "default org scope",
431: 			input:         `id=foobar;actions=read`,
432: 			scopeOverride: "o_1234",
433: 			expected: Grant{
434: 				scope: Scope{
435: 					Id:   "o_1234",
436: 					Type: scope.Org,
437: 				},
438: 				id:  "foobar",
439: 				typ: resource.Unknown,
440: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
441: 					action.Read: true,
442: 				},
443: 			},
444: 		},
445: 		{
446: 			name:          "default global scope",
447: 			input:         `id=foobar;actions=read`,
448: 			scopeOverride: "global",
449: 			expected: Grant{
450: 				scope: Scope{
451: 					Id:   "global",
452: 					Type: scope.Global,
453: 				},
454: 				id:  "foobar",
455: 				typ: resource.Unknown,
456: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
457: 					action.Read: true,
458: 				},
459: 			},
460: 		},
461: 		{
462: 			name:   "bad user id template",
463: 			input:  `id={{superman}};actions=create,read`,
464: 			userId: "u_abcd1234",
465: 			err:    `unknown template "{{superman}}" in grant "id" value`,
466: 		},
467: 		{
468: 			name:   "good user id template",
469: 			input:  `id={{    user.id}};actions=create,read`,
470: 			userId: "u_abcd1234",
471: 			expected: Grant{
472: 				scope: Scope{
473: 					Id:   "o_scope",
474: 					Type: scope.Org,
475: 				},
476: 				id: "u_abcd1234",
477: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
478: 					action.Create: true,
479: 					action.Read:   true,
480: 				},
481: 			},
482: 		},
483: 		{
484: 			name:      "bad account id template",
485: 			input:     `id={{superman}};actions=create,read`,
486: 			accountId: "apw_1234567890",
487: 			err:       `unknown template "{{superman}}" in grant "id" value`,
488: 		},
489: 		{
490: 			name:      "good account id template",
491: 			input:     `id={{    account.id}};actions=create,read`,
492: 			accountId: "apw_1234567890",
493: 			expected: Grant{
494: 				scope: Scope{
495: 					Id:   "o_scope",
496: 					Type: scope.Org,
497: 				},
498: 				id: "apw_1234567890",
499: 				actions: map[action.Type]bool{
500: 					action.Create: true,
501: 					action.Read:   true,
502: 				},
503: 			},
504: 		},
505: 	}
506: 
507: 	_, err := Parse("", "")
508: 	require.Error(t, err)
509: 	assert.Equal(t, "grant string is empty", err.Error())
510: 
511: 	_, err = Parse("", "{}")
512: 	require.Error(t, err)
513: 	assert.Equal(t, "no scope ID provided", err.Error())
514: 
515: 	for _, test := range tests {
516: 		t.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {
517: 			assert := assert.New(t)
518: 			require := require.New(t)
519: 			scope := "o_scope"
520: 			if test.scopeOverride != "" {
521: 				scope = test.scopeOverride
522: 			}
523: 			grant, err := Parse(scope, test.input, WithUserId(test.userId), WithAccountId(test.accountId))
524: 			if test.err != "" {
525: 				require.Error(err)
526: 				assert.True(strings.Contains(err.Error(), test.err), err.Error())
527: 			} else {
528: 				require.NoError(err)
529: 				assert.Equal(test.expected, grant)
530: 			}
531: 		})
532: 	}
533: }

(End of file - total 533 lines)
